body_html:
BEARING 315

Newsletter of Northwest Orienteering

SEPTEMBER/OCTOBER 1982

Cover photo: [no caption]

"NEPALESE 30-DAY" Main Event for Sept. 8 Meeting

On September 8, 7 pm at the Green Lake Library, Harry Morgan, intrepid COC member, will narrate a slide show about his journey last winter to Nepal and India. Entitled "Orienteering Annapurna (or the Nepalese 30-day)", the slide program will depict a 200-mile trek around the Annapurna Range in the Himalayas. Also included will be a side trip to the semi-tropical Royal Chitwan National Park for some wild animal trekking and viewing. Don't miss this excursion into the world's highest mountains and visit in a most intriguing culture.

Program for the Sept. 8 meeting:

  • 7:00-7:30 pm - Slide show on Nepal
  • 7:30-8:00 - Bylaws Revision
  • 8:00-8:15 - More about Cle Elum two-day event and Mountain Marathon
  • 8:15-8:30 - Report: "How are we doing financially"
  • 8:30-9:00 - Refreshments and idea sharing

Highlights of Bylaws Revisions

Many words; a few important changes:

  • Term of membership changed from old method to straight 12 months from time of payment. (Article 3, Section 4)
  • Term of office for officers changed to coincide with fiscal year. (Article 4, Section 3)
  • Changes annual meeting from February to November. (Article 5, Section 2)
  • Institute balloting by mail. (Article 4, Section 5)

Did you feel cheated last time you paid your dues in May and got a membership which lasted until December 31? And then found out about the person who paid dues in July and got a membership which ran until December 31 of the following year? Inequities and absurdities and poor grammar will hopefully be banished with revised Bylaws. Please come and vote the old into oblivion and institute a better set of Bylaws by which we govern the Club.

Schedule of Events

General information: Unless otherwise noted, novice, intermediate and advanced courses are available at all regional meets. Normal registration and start times for Cascade OC (Seattle area) events are from 10:00 am to 12:00 noon. Normal registration and start times for events by the Nisqually Orienteers (Tacoma and Lacey areas) are from 12:00 noon to 2:00 pm. The entry fee for local club events is usually $1.50 for club members and $2.50 for non-members. Novice courses at Nisqually Orienteers events are only $1.00. Regional and national events (Class B and Class A) usually have a higher entry fee and awards are given to winners in different categories. Instruction for beginners is always available at all events. A limited number of compasses are available on a rental basis at registration.

  • Saturday, Aug 28 - Woodland Park Relays. The last of the summer park series. Teams of 3 -- bring your own or simply come and join others who need teammates. Awards for winners and for team with most original and amusing name. Registration begins at 10 am in picnic shelter at S end of park. Instruction and novice course for individuals from 10 am; mass relay start soon after 11. Info: Dan Waugh, 365-1810.
  • Sunday, Aug 29 - Ft. Steilacoom Co. Park, Tacoma. Registration and starts, 12 noon-2 pm. Beginner and intermediate courses; advanced training course a multi-leg one with split times taken to compare route choices. Contact Clayton Smith, 474-5675, for details.
  • Wednesday, Sept 8 - Special program on Nepal and club meeting. 7 pm at Green Lake Library, 7364 E. Greenlake Drive N. Featuring Harry Morgan's slides of his Nepal trip followed by voting on proposed bylaws revisions (see pages 9-11 of this issue) and general club meeting concerning upcoming events. New ideas are always welcome.
  • Sat/Sun, Sept 11-12 - Black Mountain, B.C. Two-day, Class A meet organized by OABC. At Cypress Bowl Provincial Park, West Vancouver. Take Highway #1 for 5 km west of Taylor Way. Registration is at Downhill Ski Parking Lot. For info call John Collings (604) 984-9897 or Duncan Wyllie (604) 988-2806.
  • Sat/Sun, Sept 18-19 - Cle Elum Ridge. O Festival and Washington Championships. Details and registration info on insert.
  • Sat/Sun, Sept 25-26 - Calero Park, San Jose, Calif. Class A meet, California championships, on new IOF std. map. For details, call Clayton, 474-5675. Those interested in going to this meet in a rented van please contact Clayton Smith, 474-5675.
  • Sat/Sun, Oct 2-3 - First Cascade Mountain Marathon. In proposed Cougar Lakes Wilderness area, E of Mt. Rainier. Details on pages 3-4.
  • Saturday, Oct 9 - Orienteering Biathlon. Novice/intermediate cross country and bicycle courses in Woodland Park and Ballard. Details on page 12.
  • Sat/Sun, Oct 9-10 - Kamloops, B.C. Western Canadian Championships. Info: Afan Jones, 732-5129 (B.C. tel.).
  • Sunday, Oct 17 - Wonderwood Park, Lacey. Regular novice courses; score event for advanced. Registration and starts, 12-2 pm. Info: Pat Malloy, 491-0861.
  • Monday, Oct 18 - Executive Board meeting, 7 pm, location to be announced.
  • Sunday, Oct 24 - Farrell-McWhirter Park, Redmond. Fun courses for all levels, including something special for advanced runners. Park located at 19200 NE 102 Pl.; registration and starts, 10-12 am.

Orienteering class at community college

An orienteering class is being offered this fall at Fort Steilacoom Community College in Tacoma. The class will consist of three Tuesday evening sessions beginning October 5 and four Saturday sessions (some of the Saturday sessions can be conducted on Sundays if the class members so choose). The class will present orienteering as family fun and fitness as well as a sport for individual competition and enjoyment. Families can take the class at a special group rate. Contact Fort Steilacoom Community College for more information and to register: 964-6500 or 964-6633.

News Flash

Annichen Kringstad, the current World Champion and the 1981 Swedish Athlete of the Year, has tentatively agreed to come to Seattle early in October in conjunction with the Scandinavia Today program and our orienteering biathlon. The trip is contingent on finding sponsorship, working out a satisfactory schedule. We hope that she will be here the week of October 3-9 (possibly even arriving in time for the Mountain Marathon) and will be able to present a clinic as well as engage in promotional activity for the October 9 event. A special announcement will be mailed if and when details are confirmed.

First Cascade Mountain Marathon (October 2-3, 1982)

In the proposed Cougar Lakes Wilderness area, E. of Mt. Rainier. Sponsored by the Cascade Orienteering Club.

This is a two-day mountain navigation competition designed to test map-reading, navigation skill and endurance in the traverse of difficult and unknown hill country.

Competitors enter as teams of two and must maintain contact with each other throughout the event. A topographic map (based on the unpublished USGS 1:24,000 scale map) will be provided with the location of a number of checkpoints that must be visited. Participants must carry food, clothing and equipment necessary for a two-day hike in autumn conditions; camp will be established at the end of day 1's course. As with the event on which this competition is modeled -- the Karrimor International Mountain Marathon held annually in Great Britain -- there are courses for different levels of skill and fitness:

  • A "competitive" course of up to 40 km total for two days
  • A "recreational" course of up to 25 km

For both courses, ability to use map and compass is a prerequisite and a high level of fitness is recommended.

Location: Proposed Cougar Lakes Wilderness east of Mt. Rainier. From Seattle, drive Rte. 410 thru Enumclaw and 20 mi beyond Chinook Pass to Bumping River Rd. where go right, continue to end of pavement at Bumping Lk. dam. Continue 2.5 mi to junction, take left fork (Deep Creek Rd., No. 162, not marked), go 7 mi to end where park at USFS Deep Creek Campground. Driving time from Seattle, 3+ hr. Start area is 1.5 mi up trail to right at Little Twin Sister Lake (allow 1 hr for hike).

Terrain: Open and gently rolling meadows, reasonably open and somewhat more dense forest, many large and small alpine lakes; elevation, 4500-6500 ft.

Schedule: For both courses, starts day 1 from 10:00 to 11:00 am; day 2 starts from 8 am at the campsite at S end of course. Finish day 2 at Little Twin Sister Lake (est. around 2 pm).

Required equipment (will be checked by organizers at start):

  • Each team must carry: tent or bivvy sacks, first aid kit, food for 2 days and cooking gear (fires may/may not be allowed)
  • Individuals must carry: sleeping bag, suitable warm clothing and rain gear, hat, gloves, emergency food, flashlight, whistle, and compass
  • Plan for worst weather (rain/snow), hope for best
  • Purified water will be available at campsite, end of day 1, but carry some during competition as well

Miscellaneous:

  1. All participants must check in at finish whether or not they have completed the course.
  2. Participants under 18 must have permission of guardian to compete and must be on team with person over 18.
  3. If you need a teammate, we will try to match you up with a person of equal ability: enclose half of entry fee and give brief statement of experience.
  4. Prizes will be awarded.
  5. More information? Call Harry Morgan, 283-6498 (H) or Carol Johnson, 632-6682 (H).

This is a great opportunity to test and improve your mountain navigation skills at what can be the most beautiful time of year in the Cascades. The bugs are gone, there should still be some huckleberries, and the fall colors will be out. A weekend to remember; see you there!

Mountain Marathon entry / waiver form (as printed; some text illegible)

Mail to: Harry Morgan, 203 8th Avenue N, Seattle, WA 98109.

Enclose entry fee of $8.00 (checks payable to Cascade Orienteering Club) per team; deadline September 28. Teams should identify any desired Canadian participants prior to registration (notification strongly recommended). [Remaining conditions/waiver text partly illegible.] Guardian signature required if under 18.

Course (check one): [ ] Competitive [ ] Recreational
Name: ________________________________
Address: ______________________________

Proposed Bylaws Revisions (selected pages)

Membership dues (Article 3)

Old language (to be deleted): annual membership dues listed as single $7.00, family $10.00, associate $4.00; annual newsletter fee for non-members $3.00; calendar-year basis.

Old language (new members): New members who pay their first annual dues between Jan 1 and Jun 30 are paid through Dec 31 of that year; new members who pay between Jul 1 and Dec 31 are paid through Dec 31 of the following year.

Proposed Section 4: Dues are paid annually for a 12-month period of membership. A membership runs for 12 months beginning with the first of the month following the month in which the dues are received.

Proposed Section 5: Any member desiring to resign from the Club shall submit his resignation in writing to the Secretary who shall present it to the Executive Board for action.

Meetings / annual meeting / mail ballot (Article 5)

Mail ballot proposed (Section 5): Those items requiring a vote of the Club membership, such as election of officers and changes in Bylaws, may be handled by mail ballot. A minimum return of votes by 10% of the paid members is required to validate the election. A mailed ballot must be signed by the voter.

Proposed: annual meeting in November (replacing February wording in the annual meeting section).

Coordinators and committees (Article 7)

Proposed new Section 1 (replaces Public Relations Coordinator language): Promptly after taking office, the President shall appoint a Publicity Coordinator whose duties shall include general promotion, and general and specific event publicity.

Proposed new Section 2: Promptly after taking office, the President shall appoint a Membership Coordinator whose duties shall be to maintain an accurate and up to date listing of members and interested non-members and notify those whose membership has expired.

Proposed new Section 3: Promptly after taking office, the President shall appoint an Event Publicity Coordinator whose duties shall be to inform the media and other interested parties of coming Club events and results of past events.

Proposed new Section 3 (Fundraising): Promptly after taking office, the President shall appoint a Fundraising Coordinator whose duties shall include contacting potential sponsors, soliciting donations, or otherwise acquiring money for the Club.

Section 4 (revised for grammar only): Promptly after taking office, the President shall appoint a Mapping Coordinator whose duties shall be to identify and review locations suitable for Club events, and obtain or prepare suitable maps.

Training / auditing / newsletter / amendments (later articles)

Training Coordinator (revised for grammar only): President appoints a Training Coordinator; duties include organizing and conducting lectures and workshops teaching orienteering skills.

Auditing Committee (proposed): An Auditing Committee of two members shall be appointed by the President at the Club's annual meeting; duty is to audit the Treasurer's accounts at the close of the fiscal year and report at the first Executive Board meeting following the annual meeting; audit summary published for general membership.

Newsletter (proposed): "This information shall be disseminated in a timely fashion under the guidance of the Executive Board."

Amendment of bylaws (proposed new Article 11): These bylaws may be amended by a two-thirds majority of those members voting, providing that the amendment has been mailed to the membership at large and that at least 10% of the eligible members cast votes.

Disbanding (Article 12): In the event of the disbanding of the Club, any remaining assets of the Club shall become the property of the United States Orienteering Federation.

Five Mile Fun Run & Orienteering Biathlon (Honoring SCANDINAVIA TODAY)

Please return to NORTHWEST CENTER FOR THE RETARDED by OCTOBER 7, 1600 West Armory Way, Seattle, Washington 98119. Make checks payable to Northwest Center for the Retarded.

Fees:

  • Five Mile Fun Run: $6.00
  • Biathlon Orienteering: foot $6.00; bicycle $6.00; both $10.00
  • Combination Run & Biathlon: $10.00

Declaration of release (abridged; some wording hard to read): In consideration of acceptance of entry, the signer waives and releases claims for damages against NWCCR or Cascade Orienteering Club, officers/volunteers/agents/co-sponsoring groups, and acknowledges the risks; also releases subsequent use of photo; attests to being physically fit and trained.

People under 18: statement of release / permission by parent or guardian (signature + date).

More mugs: Please reserve ____ more mugs at $3.00 each. Total from mugs $. Total enclosed $.

Event notes:
This special event in conjunction with the Ballard Oktoberfest honors "Scandinavia Today." It is co-sponsored by the Northwest Center for the Retarded which receives a substantial portion of the meet entry fees. Early registration fee is noted on the form above. Day-of-event registration costs $8.50 which covers entry in both the foot orienteering at Woodland Park and the bicycle orienteering thru Ballard's environs. Prizes will be awarded by local Ballard merchants and consist of sports equipment and clothing. Each entry (group or individual) will receive a mug.

Start times:

  • On-foot event in Woodland Park: 11 am
  • On-bicycle event: 1 pm; meet at Rainier Bank parking lot at 2054 NW 56th in Ballard
  • Beginner's instruction: 10 am at Woodland Park and 12 noon at the Rainier Bank site mentioned above
  • The fun run (which is a separate event) begins at 9 am

Time Dated Material (mailing box)

Time Dated Material

Cascade Orienteering Club
PO Box 31375
Seattle, WA 98103

[Bulk mail/postage box present - details illegible]
